Add FSM proof sketch, fix logical structure claims, create large-scale experiments

Formal proof sketch establishing transformers as finite-state machines
(citing Merrill 2022, Weiss 2021, Hahn 2020, Pérez 2021). Corrects
overstated claims that embeddings "cannot represent logical structure"
to the more accurate framing: embeddings encode logical structure with
non-uniform fidelity, under-representing predicates relative to entities.

Also creates 5 large-scale experiment scripts (8x8x8 grid, expanded
taxonomies, etc.) for replication with higher statistical power.

commit.message:
Fix iOS carousel swipe with native touch event handlers

CSS touch-action:pan-x is ignored by iOS Safari when a vertically-
scrollable ancestor exists. Attach non-passive touchmove listener
directly to the carousel DOM node — determines horizontal vs vertical
intent after 5px movement, calls preventDefault() to claim the gesture
from the page scroll, then updates scrollLeft manually. touchend snaps
to the nearest card. Also removes deprecated -webkit-overflow-scrolling
and changes overflow-x to scroll for consistent cross-browser behavior.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Sync dialogue display with scene transition blackout

SceneTransitionEvent and StoryLineReadEvent fire in the same frame,
causing dialogue to change while the scene is still mid-transition.

Fix: DialogueController now listens to SceneTransitionEvent and sets
a flag. When the next StoryLineReadEvent arrives with that flag set,
dialogue display is deferred by BlackoutDuration (0.65s) — the exact
moment the overlay is fully black and the background has already
swapped. Scene and text are then revealed together as the overlay
fades out.

BlackoutDuration is now a public const on SceneTransitionOverlay so
all systems share the single source of truth.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf(parser): eliminate subshells from generate_plan hot path

generate_plan was spawning 4 $() subshells per call. Each subshell
forked a new Zsh process with VM_ALIAS_MAP empty and _VM_ALIAS_MAP_BUILT
unset, re-triggering load_vm_types on every call. 100 rapid plans took
24479ms (~245ms each).

- Add _extract_vm_names_direct: writes results into a named variable in
  the current shell so _ensure_alias_map runs once and persists
- Rewrite generate_plan to inline detect_intent, extract_filter, and
  extract_flags as case statements using ${input:l} (zero forks)
- Zero subshells in the entire hot path

Result: 100 plans 24479ms → 251ms (97x faster), green ✓ in test suite

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix HD type + Tropical rising: timezone conversion and design date iteration Root causes found and fixed: 1. TIMEZONE: sweph.julday() received local birth time as UTC. Added geo-tz package to convert local time → UT using lat/lon timezone lookup. (e.g., Singapore UTC+8: 6:10 PM local = 10:10 AM UT — 8-hour shift moves ascendant ~120°, turning Sagittarius into Aries) 2. DESIGN DATE: Newton-Raphson sign was inverted (jdDesign -= error/speed instead of +=). This caused the iteration to diverge, placing the design Sun in a completely wrong gate position, corrupting HD type. Also increased iterations to 10 with convergence check. 3. HOUSE CUSPS: Added h.data?.houses?.[n-1] fallback in sweCusp() for old sweph format on Render where cusps are in h.data.houses array. Other changes in this commit: - PDF button: sticky bottom-right, only shown after all content loads - mdToHtml: wrap text after

in

tags for proper paragraph spacing - Synthesis essence prompt: request \n\n-separated paragraphs per system - summary-essence: changed from

to

for valid nested

tags Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6

commit.message:
fix: Align audio edit apply/undo/redo response with frontend (#518)

## Summary
- `_build_audio_edit_response()` returned nested structure
(`edited_audio.*`, `edit_stack_size` integer) but frontend expected flat
fields (`current_audio_url`, `duration_after`, `edit_stack` array)
- This caused "can't access property 'length', t is undefined" when
trying to apply any edit operation (cut, trim, mute)
- Also added `duration_before`/`duration_after` tracking to edit entries

## Test plan
- [x] All 25 audio edit route tests pass
- [x] Full test suite passes (2490 backend + 189 Playwright)
- [ ] Verify edit operations work in production

@coderabbitai ignore

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Fix Dockerfile: use apk for Alpine-based hassio base image

The ghcr.io/hassio-addons/base image is Alpine Linux, not Ubuntu.
Replace apt-get with apk and update package names accordingly:
  python3-pip    → py3-pip
  libssl-dev     → openssl-dev
  python3-venv   → removed (python3 -m venv is built-in)
  musl-dev       → added (required for C extensions on Alpine)

commit.message:
Fix pitcher small-sample bias inflating bullpen quality

Pitcher arsenal query now prefers most recent season with 200+ pitches
instead of using tiny spring training samples. Added small-sample regression
for pitchers (<500 pitches regressed toward .315 league avg). Dropped NaN
xwOBA rows from weighted averages. Fixes KC bullpen xwOBA from .272 to .301
(Alex Lange had 20 pitches at .012 xwOBA inflating their projection).

commit.message:
Add adjusted fit score with stem/spacer optimization

Simulates stem length changes (±30mm) and headset spacer adjustments
(−15mm to +25mm) to find the best achievable fit for each bike.
Shows stock fit and adjusted fit side by side. Detail row shows the
specific stem/spacer change needed. Dramatically improves the number
of viable options: 53 bikes at 80%+ adjusted vs 2 at stock.

commit.message:
Forge: inactivity as one compound virtual game (INACTIVITY_DECAY^N) in set average

Missed matches produce a single virtual game factor of 0.85^N that gets averaged
with real game factors. More misses = exponentially worse factor, but real games
dilute it. Final inactivity uses same compound factor as a standalone virtual set.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

test(cli): lintCmd and compileCmd happy-path + flag validation coverage (#27)

Adds cli_test.go with 6 tests for the Cobra command wrappers that were
previously completely untested at the CLI layer:

lintCmd (3 tests):
  - TestLint_ValidSpec_DinosaurRegistry: lint the canonical example spec → "OK"
  - TestLint_ValidSpec_FleetManager: lint the full-capability fleet-manager spec → "OK"
  - TestLint_ValidSpec_Scaffolded: lint renderScaffold output → "OK"
    (additional regression guard for scaffold→lint round-trip)

compileCmd (3 tests):
  - TestCompileCmd_MissingOutputFlag: without --output → returns wrapped error
  - TestCompileCmd_ValidSpec: with --output → "Compiled" message + main.go + go.mod generated
  - TestCompileCmd_WithFileRegistry: --registry flag wires FileRegistry correctly

Note: lintCmd's invalid-spec path (which calls os.Exit(1)) cannot be tested
in-process without killing the test suite. The error path is covered at the
compiler.ParseWithSchema level in tsc/compiler/validation_test.go.

commit.message:
refactor: Pluggable strategy interface — TradingStrategy ABC replaces hardcoded if/else branches

Introduce TradingStrategy ABC with BaselineStrategy and MeanReversionStrategy
as self-contained, stateless, picklable classes. Central registry eliminates
40+ ternary branches across 7 files. Adding a third strategy now requires only
a new subclass and one registry entry — zero downstream changes.

- Phase 1-2: ABC, StrategyResult dataclass, registry (get_strategy, get_strategy_keys)
- Phase 3: backtest.py — 13 ternaries replaced with get_strategy_keys()
- Phase 4: technical_analyzer.py — new calculate_score_for_strategy(), old method delegates
- Phase 5: html_reporter.py + journal.py — strategy-driven loops
- Phase 6-7: SwingTrader.process_symbol() + worker functions use strategy loop
- Phase 8: Removed _process_baseline, _process_mr, _worker_process_baseline, _worker_process_mr

Result dict shape and MongoDB schema unchanged (backward compatible).
408 tests passing, lint clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
